Let's start by giving you an overview of how you can create a keyword list. So what you'll see on the screen here is how I generally set this up when I'm doing my research phase. On the right-hand side I have a text file, left-hand side I have my browser, and I'm just going to start with what I think is an obvious search, and then from that I'm going to let Google and advertisers and search results give me more information. So rather than have you watch me do this step by step and hear me pounding on the keyboard, what I'll do is I'll give you an overview and then I'll show you sort of along the way what it starts to look like when we build it out. But first I want you to understand how I do it. I've entered in this phrase, learn email marketing. This is my starting point. So I'm going to add that to my text file, and then if I click in here you'll see that I get search tips from, or search suggestions from Google. I'm not going to try to capture all of these right now and get led on a wild goose chase. I can find these at the bottom as well. So if I go to the bottom of the search results I can see the search is related. So I already know they're there. So I will get to those last. What I want to do first is I want to start looking at the text in the ads that show up. I am searching for learn email marketing, but immediately I'm seeing ads that are not specific to learning email marketing, which is a bit of a warning sign to me. So I might look at these. This one's local, EVM's University of Vermont. So there's some local search in here as well. I'm starting to see some other terms, newsletters, something to be aware of. Learn advanced email marketing. I think that's interesting. Email marketing strategy. Also interesting, email marketing tactics. So now I have a few things that I might add to the list. So what I'm going to do is, so you don't hear me pounding on the keyboard, I'll pause, I'll add these to my list, and then I'll keep recording and you can see how I build this list out. Okay, I'm back. What you can see is just from these ads and from the initial search results, I've started to create a short list. I don't know yet if I would bid on any of these. This is just, this is really a brainstorming exercise at this point. One thing I did notice though is I'm starting to see some of the words that are creeping up some of the active words. So learn is one, create is another, build might be another. So later as we start to reorganize this list, that's where I'll start to bring things together is by those active verbs. But for now, I've created, it's a good start to the list, but I want to look and see where there's some other opportunities. So this people also ask box is a good place to start. It looks like I have most of this already learning email marketing. I've seen a negative here, definitely free. So I want to add that to my list, which I'll do later. Develop an email marketing strategy that's interesting versus create or build or learn. So I'll include that in my list as well. I will continue to look through this first page of search results. Google has said these are the most relevant results for the search of for learning email marketing. So I should study them. That's really my job at this point, see what's showing up and why. This is an interesting article from Medium. We can see the 14 best sites to improve your email marketing skills. That would be a great resource to go look at and see like how is this being talked about, what keyword phrases are they using. And then scroll down to the bottom. Of course, we have more ads at the bottom. You can see who else is advertising. And we get to this list here of all the related search terms. And this is really a great place to do brainstorm because for each of these that I click on, I will go get to do this exercise over and over again. So I can see other negatives as well like PDF. I would like to get rid of tips. I think I might want to get rid of too. I'm not entirely sure. When I say get rid of, I mean add as negatives how to do. How to email marketing might be something I would include. I'm not sure about email marketing tools. At that point, that tells me that somebody who's looking for something that's sort of adjacent to what I'm talking about. So I just want to be aware of that. I might even use tools or tools as a negative just because I don't want to get that traffic people looking for email service providers. So there's a wealth of information on this, just on this single page. So what I'm going to do now is I'm going to pause again. I'm going to go through what I have so far and I'm going to build out my keyword list on the right just from this first page of content. Okay, I'm back. My list on the right is by no means exhaustive. I might spend hours building this list out, visiting other pages, clicking through to each page. This could take anywhere from a small amount of time would be two to three hours to six to eight hours. Don't try to do this all in one day. It's very difficult and the quality of your work diminishes rapidly. Work in an hour, maybe two hours at a time at the absolute maximum. You'll find that you get the majority of your benefit early. You sort of see some connections and then after that it starts to taper off. So what you'll see now, well just for the sake of argument say that I've reached the point where I feel like I have my whole list that I want to start working with. And you'll notice in my list now that there's a formatting change that I've made and that formatting change is that I've eliminated spaces in between some of these. So that is the signal for me in this file, this text file and you can do it with anything. But that's the signal to me that the things that are connected, there's less space between them, belong together in some way. And this is a part of the written module that I think that was confusing where I said organized by search query. What I mean by that is now I need to go through this entire list and say which of these things are related. So learn email marketing is different than email marketing strategy which is different than email marketing tactics. Now we get into a new verb, create email newsletters. So that gets me thinking about the differences between learn, create, build, automate. Now we're starting to see some verbs improve. So now I have an ingredient, I'm starting to think about an ingredient in my keyword list building which is the initial verb. After that it's looking more deeply at the intent. So how would we organize this? So this group that I've just started to put together is really organized around email lists building. So this was a search that was in their build targeted email lists or learn email list building. Again we're mixing the verb in here too, learn and build. So I have to start making some decisions about how I want to organize my ad groups. Do I want to organize it by the noun email list or by the verb build, learn, improve. There is no right answer to that. That's a decision that I have to make. I generally find myself organizing by the nouns that the intent and I generally will organize things like you can see at the bottom here where I have. This is all, even though the word learning doesn't appear, this is all learning based. So email marketing course and email marketing class and email marketing training or email marketing tutorial. To me this is a certain category of search that's looking for training. It's looking for an actual course or class that they can go take. I don't know if they want a 1099 course from Udemy or if they're interested in a much deeper dive that's more expensive. I have no idea but I do know that that's something that I would be interested in testing. Now if an ad for something that I'm offering for hundreds of dollars shows up next to an ad for Udemy that's for 1099 which shows up to something from HubSpot that's free, well that's going to be challenging. For example, if I had a multi-page pre-sell site that was a free course that led to a product I might be more interested now to think, okay, I can monetize these or this is a group that I can compete with because I have an offer that's similar or better from the search perspective. If somebody sees the ad and I can say free seven day email marketing course or training or tutorial or class that's very competitive even against the 1099. It doesn't matter to me that it leads to an expensive course over many multiple hundreds of dollars, that's fine because I know I'm going to do the work on the front end to get there. So this is how we start to build out lists and how we start to organize them. What I'm looking for in this list are things that are different and then when I find something that's different I'm trying to find if there are other things in the list that belong with it. So how to an email marketing versus improve email marketing, those two things don't seem related to me. Yes, it's the same noun, email marketing, but that's not really what I think connects them. And I'm looking for things that connect them so that I can group them together. So if I look at email marketing campaigns, this is probably the best example to show you how I begin to build these out. So email marketing campaigns is more general than create email marketing campaigns. So in this circumstance, in this situation, I'm less likely to bid on email marketing campaigns without having the word create in there because create to me signify some intent. Now, I could also have, I noticed down here that improve email marketing. Well, that could be improve email marketing campaigns. So I might copy this and from there I might add it here and then add campaigns. Now, these are separate. Again, these are still separate. Improving email marketing is general. Improving an email marketing campaigns. That it's a little more specific and I like that specificity. So now what I'm starting to notice is I have this, I have a broad category around doing things with email marketing campaigns, creating them, improving them, maybe optimizing them. We've got up here, we've got this optimization for email open rates. Like now, now I'm starting to develop the language that I can use and that that language is the core. It really comes down to things. If I thinking out loud, there are probably three things that matter most. There's the verb, like what is the action the person is asking to do. There's the noun, which is what is it about. And then there are the modifiers like optimization. So you can start, when you start putting these together and mixing and matching, you get a lot of really interesting combinations so that when I see this whole list that just started with learn email marketing and now I start seeing strategy, tactics, list building, optimization, nurturing, I can begin to mix and match these together and organize them so that I could have just for example, if I wanted to build out improve email marketing, I might have improve email marketing strategy, improve email marketing tactics, improve email marketing optimization, improve email open rates, like I can mix and match lots of these things. Doesn't mean I'm going to use any of them. It certainly doesn't mean I'm going to use any of them to start. But it is my way of building out through my what I would call my keyword universe, which I will then feedback into Google's keyword planner and have it give me an estimate and let me know really where was I on track and where was I off track. Many of these are going to have little or no traffic and I can just eliminate them and realize, okay, it was an interesting idea, but it is better to go a little bit broader or a lot broader. In fact, it's better to go a lot broader and then see where the actual traffic is. As long as it's organized intelligently, that becomes much easier. And this note on the right really is how I begin to organize. I don't use spreadsheets and I don't make it more complicated from my perspective than it needs to be. If you're very comfortable working in spreadsheets, I've seen lots of spreadsheets that organize keywords very well. It's particularly good if you're going to later pull the data into that and have a sense of what you've been on and the results that you get. I would rather just export that later from Google ads than try to set it up now. But again, that's me talking about how I do this after having done it tens of thousands of times. So you may find when you first get started, when you have four or five, however long it takes you to get the four or five profitable keyword phrases over your testing some at the same time, you may want to have a spreadsheet that you update and it forces you to go in and put in the data that says this is performing well or this is not performing well. You may find, for example, that if you use just conversion tracking in Google ads and it triggers that conversion that you do actually need to go back and do this math anyway in a spreadsheet, because the conversion, unless you have a numerical value that you've assigned to it with no upsells or you just have a single product, then it can calculate the numbers for you. But if not, if you need to put your total amount of money that you've made from your front end sale and various upsells, or if there's a time lag where you have to do that anyway, or if there's cost per lead that you have to factor in, like lots of these things would suggest that putting this into a spreadsheet is very useful. I don't do that, but that does not mean that you should do that. What we care about, however, at this stage is that we have somewhere between 25 and 75 potential search terms that we can use. We're going to take those and put them in the Google's keyword planner. And once we put them in the keyword planner and get some estimates for where the traffic is, we're going to narrow down our choice and we're going to choose one keyword phrase that we think gives us the best opportunity initially. We may be wrong and we may quickly discover that that phrase is not profitable or it's not as profitable or there's not nearly enough traffic. Lots of things can happen, but we won't know until we actually make that decision. We run some traffic to it. We get a result. And then we have the rest of our list that we continue to work through that gives us the opportunity to keep going through this process. One very focused keyword phrase at a time.
